entity.name: `<svg>` (Inline SVG)
entity.description: Embed SVG vector graphics directly in markup
entity.descriptionHtml: Embed SVG vector graphics directly in markup
entity.descriptionClean: Embed SVG vector graphics directly in markup

example.codeHighlighted: <svg> <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="50" /> </svg>

webFeature.description_html: The SVG image format, represented by the <svg> element, creates two-dimensional vector graphics with declarative or scripted interaction and animation.
webFeature.name: SVG

entity.name: Browser support
entity.description: Browser support, compatibility, and consistency issues.
entity.descriptionHtml: Browser support, compatibility, and consistency issues.
entity.descriptionClean: Browser support, compatibility, and consistency issues.

webFeature.description_html: The clip-path CSS property and SVG attribute set the visible area of an element. Everything outside the area will be hidden.
webFeature.name: clip-path

webFeature.description_html: The mask CSS property (and several longhand properties) partially or completely hides an element according to the shape and depth of an image.
webFeature.name: Masks

entity.name: Lack of documentation
entity.description: Issues due to missing, incomplete, unclear, or outdated documentation or code examples.
entity.descriptionHtml: Issues due to missing, incomplete, unclear, or outdated documentation or code examples.
entity.descriptionClean: Issues due to missing, incomplete, unclear, or outdated documentation or code examples.

webFeature.description_html: The backdrop-filter CSS property applies graphical effects such as blurring or color shifting to the area behind an element.
webFeature.name: backdrop-filter

webFeature.description_html: The min-content and max-content CSS keywords represent the smallest and largest intrinsic sizes of an element.
webFeature.name: min-content and max-content

resources.title: Understanding CSS corner-shape and the Power of the Superellipse

webFeature.description_html: The corner-shape CSS property sets the shape of an element's corners when using border-radius, allowing for shapes other than rounded corners. For example, corner-shape: squircle is a shape in between a square and rounded corner.
webFeature.name: corner-shape

example.codeHighlighted: p { display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; overflow: hidden; }

webFeature.description_html: The line-clamp CSS property limits the text in a block container to a certain number of lines. The prefixed -webkit-line-clamp is widely supported but only works with -webkit-box-orient: vertical in combination with display: -webkit-box or display: -webkit-inline-box.
webFeature.name: line-clamp

webFeature.description_html: The clip-path CSS property and SVG attribute set the visible area of an element. Everything outside the area will be hidden.
webFeature.name: clip-path

entity.name: Animations
entity.description: Issues related to UI animations across all technologies, regardless of technology used to implement them (CSS animations, Lottie, etc)
entity.descriptionHtml: Issues related to UI animations across all technologies, regardless of technology used to implement them (CSS animations, Lottie, etc)
entity.descriptionClean: Issues related to UI animations across all technologies, regardless of technology used to implement them (CSS animations, Lottie, etc)

example.code:
border-shape: shape(
  from top left,                            
  hline to calc(100% - var(--arrow-size)), 
  line to right center,                           
  line to calc(100% - var(--arrow-size)) bottom,
  hline to left,      
  close                                      
);
example.codeHighlighted: border-shape: shape( from top left, hline to calc(100% - var(--arrow-size)), line to right center, line to calc(100% - var(--arrow-size)) bottom, hline to left, close );

resources.title: border-shape: the future of the non-rectangular web

webFeature.description_html: The border-shape CSS property sets the geometry of the border box, changing the shape of any applicable border, border image, focus outline, or shadow.
webFeature.name: border-shape

resources.title: Leading-Trim: The Future of Digital Typesetting

example.codeHighlighted: a:has(> img) { border: 2px solid grey; }

webFeature.description_html: The :has() CSS functional pseudo-class matches an element if any of the selectors passed as parameters would match at least one element.
webFeature.name: :has()
